ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
department: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
implementing_agency: MeitY Startup Hub (MSH), Digital India Corporation
target_beneficiaries: Eligible accelerators as direct scheme partners and selected product-based or IT-based startups as downstream beneficiaries.
startup_stage: Potential technology and software-product startups requiring acceleration from product or prototype development towards commercialization and growth.
eligibility: Direct scheme partners are existing or upcoming accelerators operating in India. The scheme document requires relevant incubation or accelerator experience, startup-support track record, suitable infrastructure, mentor and investor networks, and capability to run structured cohorts. Startups participate as downstream beneficiaries through accelerators selected under cohort-specific calls.
benefits: Selected startups may receive customized acceleration services, customer and investor connections, mentoring, legal and product support, market-access assistance and matching funding support under an approved SAMRIDH cohort.
application_process: MeitY Startup Hub or its implementing arrangement issues a cohort-specific Expression of Interest or call for accelerators. Eligible accelerators apply and undergo expert evaluation. Selected accelerators then operate cohorts and select eligible startups through their governed screening process. No currently open SAMRIDH application window has been verified.
sector_evidence: Official scheme material establishes startup acceleration, incubation, innovation and funding support.
sector_reason: The scheme supports technology startups through acceleration, incubation and structured financial or market support.
startup_beneficiary_evidence: Eligible accelerators as direct scheme partners and selected product-based or IT-based startups as downstream beneficiaries.
startup_access_evidence: Support is delivered through selected accelerators or incubation centres; no currently open central application route is asserted.

ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
implementing_agency: Innovation and IPR Division, MeitY, in association with 51 incubation centres
target_beneficiaries: Designated incubation centres, technology entrepreneurs and ICT or emerging-technology startups supported through those centres.
startup_stage: Idea to proof of concept through Entrepreneur-in-Residence support; prototype development through grants; and product development or market outreach through investment support.
eligibility: The approved implementation model operated through designated incubation centres at institutes of higher learning and research organisations. Downstream support was intended for technology startups and entrepreneurs working with emerging technologies in approved thematic areas. No current application eligibility or open application window has been verified.
benefits: Financial and technical incubation support, mentoring, ecosystem linkages and stage-based startup assistance through approved TIDE 2.0 incubation centres.
application_process: During the approved implementation period, eligible entrepreneurs and startups accessed support through participating TIDE 2.0 incubation centres. The permanent scheme record does not provide a currently open central application route.
sector_evidence: Official scheme material establishes startup acceleration, incubation, innovation and funding support.
sector_reason: The scheme supports technology startups through acceleration, incubation and structured financial or market support.
startup_beneficiary_evidence: Designated incubation centres, technology entrepreneurs and ICT or emerging-technology startups supported through those centres.
startup_access_evidence: Historical TIDE 2.0 support was delivered through participating incubation centres. The approved period ended on 2024-01-31; no current central application route is asserted.
